task_completion.description: Open the draft demographics document in LibreOffice Writer
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Writer window is open displaying the 'draft_demographics.docx' file, which contains a 6-column clinical demographics table.

task_completion.description: Create a multi-level header for the Placebo group
task_completion.visual_evidence: A single merged header cell containing 'Placebo (N=142)' spans across exactly two columns (the Placebo Mean and SD columns).

task_completion.description: Create a multi-level header for the Sarilumab group
task_completion.visual_evidence: A single merged header cell containing 'Sarilumab (N=138)' spans across exactly two columns (the Sarilumab Mean and SD columns).

task_completion.description: Align all numerical data by decimal point
task_completion.visual_evidence: The numerical values in the Mean, SD, and P-value columns (the 5 rightmost columns) are vertically aligned by their decimal points. This is typically visible via decimal tab stop markers on the top ruler.

task_completion.description: Remove all vertical borders for APA style
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table has no vertical lines separating any of the columns.

The table features a thick horizontal line at the very top, a thick horizontal line at the very bottom, and a thin horizontal line separating the header rows from the data rows. There should be no horizontal lines between the standard data rows.
The LibreOffice Writer title bar shows 'table1_final.docx' or a 'Save As' dialog is visible saving the file to '/home/ga/Documents/table1_final.docx'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreOffice Writer GUI to format the table
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice Writer menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes (e.g., Table Properties, Paragraph settings) rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying the file.

integrity.description: Decimal alignment was achieved using word processor features, not manual spacing
integrity.visual_evidence: Alignment is achieved using decimal tab stops (visible on the ruler or in paragraph settings) rather than inserting multiple space characters before numbers to visually align them.

integrity.description: The original clinical data was preserved
integrity.visual_evidence: The table still contains the original clinical measures (Age, BMI, Duration of RA, CRP, DAS28-CRP, HAQ-DI Score) and their corresponding numerical values. The agent did not replace the table with an image or dummy text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is a 6-column by 7-row table containing clinical demographics. The task requires merging the 'Placebo (N=142)' header over columns 1-2 (Placebo Mean/SD) and 'Sarilumab (N=138)' over columns 3-4 (Sarilumab Mean/SD). Columns 1-5 (all numerical columns) must be aligned by decimal point. APA formatting requires removing all vertical borders, adding thick top/bottom borders, and a thin line under the header. The final file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/table1_final.docx.
